qualitative_notes: Baseline saturates the binary eval suite. The score does not mean craft-reflect is perfect — it means the evals only check output shape and the agent trivially produces that shape when following SKILL.md.

Reviewing the three outputs qualitatively reveals real weaknesses the evals do not catch: (a) Recommended-changes items map 1:1 to Issues items, so there is no prioritization or consolidation; (b) Minimal rewrite plan is a subset of Recommended changes rather than a prioritized sequence; (c) Failure modes frequently restate Issues in future tense rather than adding a distinct dimension; (d) no severity or priority labels anywhere.

This is the 'false positive' scenario described in references/eval-guide.md § 'When the scores look fine but real use does not.' The eval suite needs to be strengthened before a mutation loop can produce meaningful signal.
